text:
Yo! A new super! Man, you guys are just popping out of the woodwork these days. Lucky for you, Deadpool's here to introduce you to some peeps who might be willing to help you learn to use these new powers of yours. 

So here's the deal... First you need to figure out whether your powers are more inclined towards inventing things, casting spells, physical violence, or playing mind games. 

You got it? Okay. I am gonna introduce you to some heroes and villains that have expressed a willingness to take on an 'apprentice.' A sidekick, if you will.

The mentor you choose will determine your potential power growth as well as your access to that sweet sweet cash that let's you buy gear. Your mentor will also determine people's initial impressions of you: A mere millanious mentor will make people tend to view you a bit more warily. It's nothing you cannot overcome, though, with hard work and not caring what people think.

options.label: Tony Stark (Iron Man)
options.description:
Ah, Tony. Billionaire, Genius, Playboy, Philanthropist. If you are a hottie with a body, you can expect to learn more than science from our boy here. 

I will go ahead and let you know that Tony's got the most money to offer, so if you are a total gold digger, you might as well stop here. 

80% Paragon / 20% Renegade

options.label: Otto Octavius (Doc Ock)
options.description:
Doctor Octogonopus BLAAAUUGGH

Heh. Mid-2000s memes.

Seriously though, Otto here is a piece o work. Single-minded, ruthless, and little bit creepy, but there's no denying here's a genius.

10% Paragon / 90% Renegade

options.label: Reed Richards
options.description:
Now... I don't mean to pry, but do you think he stretches... everywhere?  Like... what's it feel like to be Mrs. Fantastic? 

Anyways, Reed's very much the absent minded professor type. He has a family, so you won't get as much one-on-one time as you might with the others. You might learn from his family too, though. 

100% Paragon / 0% Renegade

options.label: Peter Parker
options.description:
If you pick my bro Spidey, you are gonna have to share. We hang. 

Get it? Hang? 

Because of the webbing

...

Anyway, Spidey's a good kid. Most people underestimate him, but he's every but as smart as the other mentors here. 

80% Paragon / 20% Renegade

options.label: Victor Von Doom
options.description:
Doom's pretty much a badass at everything. Remember that time he became God-Emperor Doom and ruled everything? That was a wild time. 

He's a jack-of-all trades and he's a master of most of them too. He would be sorcerer supreme if he bothered to try, and he claims to be smarter than Reed. 

30% Paragon / 70% Renegade

options.label: Stephen Strange
options.description:
The actual Sorcerer Supreme Doctor Strange is arguably the most powerful magic user on Earth. He's a bit stuffy and uptight for my taste, but I suppose that comes with the territory. 

That time Doom was a God-Emperor, though, Strange was only his right hand man. Chew on that for a bit. 

90% Paragon / 10% Renegade

options.label: Wanda Maximoff
options.description:
In addition to looking almost as good in red as I do, Wanda is an immensely powerful witch as her name implies. Basically, she can do literally whatever she wants. 

You probably won't get that good. Probably.

You can come close though.

90% Paragon / 10% Renegade

options.label: Johnny Blaze (Ghost Rider)
options.description:
Johnny and his demon boddy Xanatos are a package of hellfire and steel on one sweet ride. 

They do not really have a 'home' so you will be wandering around hunting criminals while training with him.

I hope you do not have any thing you feel guilty about. 

50% Paragon / 50% Renegade

options.label: Steve Rogers (Captain America)
options.description:
America's Golden Boy! The Super Soldier! The Capscicle! 

We are not addressing the new run of him actually being a member of Hydra because that's stupid and the writers are stupid. 

Cap is one of the best hand-to-hand fighters, not in the least because he can 'see' faster than most people ... Don't ask

95% Paragon / 5% Renegade

options.label: Batroc the Leader
options.description:
Hey, another merc! He's French though. He's a prick. 

Batroc doesn't have any poewrs, but he can hang with people like Cap and Hawkeye. He's like a gold and purple pinball when he fights.

He also doesn't hesitate to use guns or explosives when he needs to. He takes on just about any job, and makes tons of money doing it. 

10% Paragon / 90% Renegade

options.label: Kevin Masters (Taskmaster)
options.description:
Perhaps the most mentor-ly mentor of the 16 available, Taskmaster has the ability to perfectly recall and replicate any combat skill he observes. This made him an excellent teacher with a wide assortment of villains and anti-heroes. 

Currently working for the US Government to clear his record, which is why he is willing to teach newbs like you. 

20% Paragon / 80% Renegade

options.label: Victor Greed (Sabertooth)
options.description:
Not as calm and pleasant as my buddy, his brother. Wolvie, but he is a beast. 

Literally.

Victor will rip your insides out just for looking at him funny. He promises not to do that to his apprentice. I think he's bored.

Or he likes grooming people in his image. 

0% Paragon / 100% Renegade

options.label: Charles Xavier
options.description:
Baldy here's probably the first person you think of when you think of psychic powers. He's one of the strongest telepaths in the world, though that's not saying all that much.

I mean really, dude cannot even get into Magneto's head.

95% Paragon / 5% Renegade

options.label: Mantis
options.description:
The Celestial Madonna herself, Mantis is a bit of a weird one, but she can beat up anyone you care to name. In addition to her scary psychic powers.

She mostly prefers to use them in subtle ways, implanting suggestions and using her precognition in combat. 

Mostly

80% Paragon / 20% Renegade

options.label: The Mandarin
options.description:
Mandarin? What's he doing here? He's not-

Oh yeah, he's got a ring for that. Well, I guess that works. He;'s not innately psychic, but ring #6 "Demonic" (Hey, I didn't name them) gives him mental powers up to and including mind control.

Dude has rings for everything. It's absurd.

10% Paragon / 90% Renegade

options.label: Emma Frost
options.description:
Ooh-la-la

Ms. Frost is considered second only to Xavier in mental power, but there are other perks to being her student. 

By which I mean money. She's the leader of the Hellfire Club. 

You're not even on her dance card.

40% Paragon / 60% Renegade

name: Powers

options.label: Genius
options.description:
Rank 1: You are the equivalent to Stephen Hawking in your chosen fields, and you can master new fields in a master of weeks. You are not necessarily going to be making breakthroughs on a regular basis, but you are always going to be the smartest person in the room.  (Except for other genius supers of course) 

Rank 2: Tony Stark was able to build the Iron Man suit in a cave! With a box of scraps! You will be able to accomplish similar feats, and your analytical mind makes you a master of planning and tactics. Even other geniuses will be unable to best you and with prep, you will be able to win most fights.

options.label: Technopathy
options.description:
Rank 1: You can control and manipulate machinery to do your bidding at range, and interface with computers without the need for clumsy devices like keyboards. All but the most heavily encrypted servers are open to you, and automated systems will be repurposed to serve you.

Rank 2: Your control over machines extends to both the macro and micro levels. You can disassemble and reassemble machinery, re-configuring it to suit your exact needs, no encryption is too secure for you to crack it, and you can create AI on the fly to control your mechanical creation.

options.label: Construction
options.description:
Rank 1: Assembling the gear that makes you a super is second-nature to you. By machining and fabricating the parts yourself, you can make more gear than your budget would otherwise suggest, and repairing it is never a problem. You save 15% on item costs, and all gear is slightly more effective.

Rank 2: You are among the greatest technicians in the world, and possibly beyond. Parts seems to appear from thin air when you need them, letting you assemble more than seems possible. You save an additional 15% on item costs, and all gear is markedly more effective.

options.label: Alien Tech
options.description:
Rank 1 (2 points): Through accidental contact, devoted study, or a reference from your mentor, you have managed to get your hands on alien technology, which you have reversed engineered to augment your own gear. The second tier of transformative items is unlocked for you. 

Rank 2 (3 points): Your mastery of alien technology is second to none. Any one-second-tier transformative item you possess has its effects boosted by an order of magnitude. The exact nature of this boost is up to your discretion.

options.label: Elemental
options.description:
Rank 1: You possess either moderate control over all elements, or the weather, or significant control over a single element. A generalized elementalist might be able to control an amount of earth or water equivalent to their own mass, while a specialized elementalist might be able to control up to a ton of matter. 

Rank 2: A generalized elementalist at Rank 2 might have the same control over all elements that a specialized elementalist has at Rank 1. A specialized elementalist at Rank 2 could summon hurricane-force winds, fire as intense as the surface of the sun, or flood an entire city. At Rank 2, weather elementalist has powers on pars with Storms.

options.label: Utility Magic
options.description:
Rank 1: Quality of Life Magic. Things like minor telekinesis, storing small items in pocket dimensions, changing your outfit on the fly, or minor illusions. Anything a mage could do casually to make his life easier probably falls into this category. 

Rank 2: You can use magic to do basically anything technology can do. Travel? Fly or Teleport. Storage? Bag of Holding. Someone shooting at you? Arcane Armor. Note that unlike Elemental Magic, you gain all these abilities from a single purchase of utility magic. To gian specialized control over two elements would require 2 purchases of Rank 1 and 2 of Elemental Magic.

options.label: Summoning
options.description:
Rank 1: You are able to bring creatures from other planes or world to do your bidding. Their exact nature is up to you, and ranks in elemental magic allows you to call elementals of your chosen type. More powerful than the basic summons available with only this magic. 

Rank 2: You are able to call gods and demons from outer planes. Beings of immense power and wisdom. Be warned: These beings are under no obligation to serve you and will require service in exchange for their boons. Rank 2 Summoning and Rank 2 Elemental Magic would allow an Earth Specialist to animate an entire mountain.

options.label: Temporal Magic
options.description:
Rank 1 (2 points): Through intense study or fortuitous innate power, you begin to unlock time magic. One of the most powerful and dangerous magical schools. Though you are not yet able to change the course of time, you can slow it down, speed it up, or even pause it for brief periods. 

Rank 2 (3 points): Time is unlocked completely for you. Travel forwards, backwards, and across timelines at will. Time magic still requires focus, so this won't make you immune to being surprised, but if they fail to kill you, you can always just rewind time and try again.

options.label: Strength
options.description:
Rank 1: You possess all-around strength at approximately twice the level a human could possibly achieve , putting your above "peak humans" like Captain America. This gives you a modest boost ins speed and durability, but not any more than a regular human could achieve. 

Rank 2: You're not quite at hulk's level, but throwing tanks through skyscrapers and leaping tall buildings in a single bound are certainly within your grasp. Be careful with those jumps though. Strength alone won't keep you from splattering when you hit the ground.

options.label: Speed
options.description:
Rank 1: Your speed, agility, and reaction times are all boosted beyond human maximums. You could easily win gold in Olympic Gymnastics with a little training for the routines, and nothing dropped within arm's reach of you ever hits the floor. Your top speeds approach 60mph.

Rank 2: You can either specialize into speed or agility. Pure pseed will enable you to run at approximately .1c. Though at those speeds perceiving your surroundings will be difficult. If you choose agility, you will be able to perceive faster than humans, able to dodge even lasers, reacting impossibly quickly. Your body needs almost no time to process data and react accordingly.

options.label: Durability
options.description:
Rank 1: Your body becomes durable, like firm rubber. Bludgeoning blows, falls from moderate heights, and small-caliber bullets will have almost no effect on you. You also gain a heightened resilience to pressure, temperature, and illness/toxins. 

Rank 2: You are virtually invulnerable: Heat, Cold, Pressure, Gunfire, Blades, Poison.. None of it is liable to even scratch you. You can hold your breath for hours on end, enabling you to survive even the vacuum of space for a time. Only exotic weaponry or powers are liable to pose a threat.

options.label: Healing Factor
options.description:
Rank 1 (2 points): Wounds that would cause a normal human to bleed out are survivable for you. You heal approximately 7 times faster than a normal human, so an accident that would hospitalize a normal human for a month would only take you 4 days to recover from. 

Rank 2 (3 points): Your healing factor is the equivalent of of Wolvie's or mine! Major injuries heal within a minute and you can regenerate from complete evisceration as long as a chunk of your body remains intact.

options.label: Telepathy
options.description:
Rank 1: You can send and receive messages directly between minds and allow communication between two non-telepathic minds by acting as a link between them. You can extract information from the mind of an unaware or unwilling individual. But those with strong wills or training may be able to resist you. 

Rank 2: Your ability to break past the mental guards of others improves, allowing you to access the minds of nearly all persons. You can also cross the boundaries of species, reading the minds of most animals, and can blur the lines between reality and imagination, planting false memories and illusions in the minds of others.

options.label: Telekinesis
options.description:
Rank 1: You can lift mentally only as much as you can lift individually: To start, you can increase this limit through mental exercise to an upper limit of approximately 4 times the human strength, and of course you can do things with telekinesis that cannot be done with raw strength.

Rank 2: With discipline and training, you can lift many tons without batting an eye. Your telekinetic strength will rival the strongest physical heroes and you will have far more versatility with it.

options.label: Precognition
options.description:
Rank 1: Whenever you sleep, meditate, or otherwise allow your mind to relax, you may experience flashes of possible futures. Imminent catastrophes may force a vision on you even when you are alert. 

Rank 2: You do not always know why, but you usually know the best course of action to take. It is not infallible, but it is much better than nothing. With focus, you can travel the possible futures and discern the actions required to bring them about. Warnings of catastrophes come sooner, and more reliably.

options.label: Domination
options.description:
Rank 1 (2 points): You can completely control the thoughts and actions of a single target, or strongly influence several: Animals are also subject to this effect, but those of strong will can resist and the effect weakens the longer the subject is away from you.

Rank 2 (3 points): You can completely control up to 5 people. Even those with iron will (for a brief time) and influence large masses of people (For example, inciting a riot in a crowd.) As long as you focus in on your thralls periodically, the effect does not decay with time.

description: Note: All Powers have a cost equal to their rank unless otherwise noted, and you must buy Rank One before buying Rank Two.

options.label: Utility Belt 1
options.description: A typical utility belt that will provide typical tools to assist in most situations. Lockpicks, A grappling hook, maybe a re-breather. You can decide exactly what it contains, within research.

options.label: Utility Belt 2
options.description: Your utility belt seems to defy the laws of physics. If there is a device that can fit in your hand that would assist you in a given task, you can probably pull it out of your utility belt

options.label: Guns 1
options.description: You have unlimited access to most guns, up to and including light machine guns, accessories, and special ammunition types are included, but it might take you a few days to get anything too exotic

options.label: Guns 2
options.description: You have access to any weapon that fires non-explosive ordinance. Your tiers of explosives also stack with this tier of guns to grant you RPGs, Rocket Launchers, etc. To mount similar weapons on your vehicle, if you have one, you can fire any kind of explosive granted by your tiers in explosives.

options.label: Shield Generator 1
options.description: You can carry a shield generator that can be triggered at will, capable of deflecting gunfire and resisting most basic elemental attacks. However, it can only be used for a few minutes at a time before it needs to recharge, and can be overwhelmed.

options.label: Shield Generator 2
options.description: Your shield is active at all times unless you deactivate it, and can withstand immense damage before being overwhelmed. Further, your shield can also withstand immense damage before being overwhelmed. Further, your shield can also provide a hermetic shield, protecting you from gases and vacuum, and provides some protection from psychic effects.

options.label: Exo Suit 1
options.description: A basic exosuit provides you with effective Rank 1 Strength, Speed, and Durability. If you already have Rank 1, this will be a moderate boost for you. If you have Rank 2, this will be negligible.

options.label: Exo Suit 2
options.description: An advanced, high-powered exosuit that grants you effective Rank 2 strength and durability, but still only grants Rank 1 speed. It also grants you flight at a max speed of 300mph and includes built-in Tier 1 Ray Weaponry. Though, you cannot use the rays in anything other than the suit.

options.label: Vehicle 1
options.description: A tier one vehicle is restricted to being land-bound and can be no larger in size than a small motor home. You can cram as much tech as you can get your hands on into it.

options.label: Vehicle 2
options.description: The X-Men's Blackbird, The Avengers' quinjet. All these and more cna be springboards for the kind of badass vehicle you can buy with Tier 2. At this level, your vehicle can also include high-end things like cloaking.

options.label: Explosives 1
options.description: Grenades, Landmines, Tripwires, Breaching charges, and basically any other "normal" kind of explosive ordinance that is not fired from a gun.

options.label: Explosives 2
options.description: You can make explosives with effects other than death. Freezing grenades, sticky grenades, gas grenades, and more. Anything you can pack into a grenade is now open to you. You can also make more advanced grenades, such as a drilling grenade, homing grenades, or cute little helicopter grenades.

options.label: Teleporter 1
options.description: You can, once per ten minutes or so, teleport anywhere within 1000 yards of yourself. You must know your destination or be able to see it, otherwise you risk teleporting yourself into a wall and killing yourself.

options.label: Teleporter 2
options.description: The cooldown on your teleport is reduced to a mere ten seconds, and you can teleport up to 1,000 miles.

options.label: Nanomachines 1
options.description: You gain an injection of of nanomachines, which can respond to your mental commands and grant you powers at need. The nanomachines are the equivalent of three points of physical powers, which can be changed at will.

options.label: Nanomachines 2
options.description: The nanomachines are both more potent and more versatile, functioning as five effective points of physical power and two points of either magic or psychic points.

options.label: Lair 1
options.description: A nice penthouse suite, A house in a surburban neighborhood, or a basement with a secret entrance from a shop above you: Contains all the amenities you need for your superhero and civilian lives.

options.label: Lair 2
options.description: A base on the level of Avengers Tower or The Batcave. You have all that you could need for your life of crime/heroism, including a place to store your vehicle if you have one in addition to sweet living quarters for your civilian life.

options.label: Ray Weaonry 1
options.description: You have access to the most basic rays: Heat and Cold. You can burn things, melt things, and vaporize things with heat or you can freeze and shatter things with cold.

options.label: Ray Weaonry 2
options.description: You can create rays with more bizarre and exotic effects, such as shrink rays, death rays, mind control rays, and more! Your potential is essentially limitless, though you will be in the end still just a guy with a gun.

options.label: Drones 1
options.description: You can design and program drones roughly up to modern technology, Small flying drones and tread crawling drones and similar. These can only perform simple tasks unless you can create AI yourself.

options.label: Drones 2
options.description: You can create advanced, humanoid drones, capable of high-powered combat or recon and controlled with specialized AI. Combat drones can be equipped with any weaponry you have purchased.

options.label: Radiation 1
options.description: Exposure to radiation has granted you a second form that only triggers under certain conditions. When activated, you gain an additional rank in all physical stats. This form can only be maintained for 10 minutes per day, and leaves you drained afterwards.

options.label: Radiation 2
options.description: You can refine the radiation exposure to either provide you with a permanent extra rank of every physical power, or boost your physical powers by two effective ranks while in your alternate form.

name: Relationships

options.label: Kitty Pryde (Shadowcat)
options.description:
Ohh. The X-Men's resident kitty cat! Miss Pryde here can be a bit catty at times, and I don't just mean in the usual ways. She can be as fond of you as the day is long, but disappear when you most expect her.

Not to say she's unreliable. Though, she's a good friend to have, and a nifty person to have your back in a fight.What with her ability to phase through things.

options.label: Anna Marie (Rogue)
options.description:
Low-Key, one of the strongest X-Men, shes got far more than just the power theft she's known for. Depending on when you get to know her, she's also got flight and super strength! 

On a personal level, she's somewhat withdrawn on account of not bieng able to touch people. But she's also sassy and no-nonsense, and more than willing to lay the beat-down on anyone who needs it.

options.label: Piotr "Peter" Rasputin (Colossus)
options.description:
Now this guy's hard! Heh

Colossus is stronger and more durable than steel. And I am not just talking about his power. He can be a bit socially awkward, but he has a big heart and even bigger shoulders. 

He's always there for his comrades, and always there for his enemies, too.

options.label: Remy Lebeau (Gambit)
options.description:
The cajun sensation that's sweeping the nation. This French-speaking mutant has a tendency to make things explode, and I am not just talking about his power either. 

He is a fiery one, and he's got a fondness for playing cards, both as weapons and for gambling. Depending on when you're coming into the scene, may be in a relationship with Rogue.

options.label: Pietro Maximoff (Quicksilver)
options.description:
Twin brother to potential mentor Wanda Maximoff, Quicksilver here has super speed and significant issues related to them. He doesn't usually let on about it, but he can get really tired of petty delays, really quickly. 

Other than that though, he's as brave and loyal a friend as you could have asked for, even if he has a tendency to rush in.

options.label: Laura Kinney (X-23)
options.description:
Technically, a female clone of ol' Wolvie, she considers herself his daughter, since he raised her for a while. 

A bit younger than the other people on this list, but she's out doing her own thing. 

Basically a smaller Wolverine, if we're being honest. She inherited a lot from her adoptive brother.

options.label: Scott Summers (Cyclops)
options.description:
On-again off-again leader of the X-Men.Most of the time Scotty here is a valiant but wark of truth, justice, and the American way, but he has his dark episodes, too.

The guy's got a strong power, and he's good in a fight, but he just cannot take a joke, y'know? He could definitely afford to lighten up, a lot.

options.label: Kurt Wagner (Nightcrawler)
options.description:
One of the most effective mutants to have in a fight. Between the skill with swords and other combat styles, and his ability to teleport, Kurt is also just a great guy to be around. 

I mean he's got issues, like anybody else, but he has generally healthy coping mechanisms and tries to be friendly to people who aren't trying to kill him.

options.label: Franklin Richards
options.description:
Can't talk about reality warpers without Franklin Richards. The son of Reed Richards and Susan Storm, this kid is stupidly powerful. He's got telepathy, telekinesis, molecular control over matter, the list goes on. He's basically a tiny god. 

Fortunately, if you get him young enough, he's mostly just telekinetic and can be guided into not being a twat when he grows up.

options.label: Slapstick
options.description:
Wow, digging deep for this one, huh?

Slapstick doesn't really warp reality outside of himself, but what he does is pretty rad nonetheless. He's a cartoon character in the real world. Hammer-space, rubber body, running off the edge of a cliff without realizing it, etc.

That applies to his behavior as well.

options.label: Doreen Allene Green (Squirrel GIrl)
options.description:
Okay. so her powers don't explicitly include breaking the fourth wall or actually warping reality, but come on, this girl? Beating Doom? I don't think so. She's like the opposite of a jobber.

Personality-wise, I hear she's rather squirrel-like. Chipper and upbeat and almost obnoxiously full of energy. Not my cup of tea. But maybe you're into that?

options.label: Wade Wilson
options.description:
Wha- Is this me? Am I on the list that I drafted? What am I doing here? 

Okay. Yes. I'm here. Sue me. I'm bored. We're already friends, right? This is just formalizing it. I kid a lot, but I'm actually useful to have in a fight. 

Especially when I have the reality stone. That's when things get really wild.

options.label: Felivia Harding (Black Cat)
options.description:
Yo, have you played the new Spider-Man game? Specifically the heist DLC? Black Cat is smokin' hot in that.

But do not let that distract you as much as it distracts my boy Spidey. She's a professional thief, and one of the best in the world. And she's good at outfoxing everyone, including the people who hired her.

options.label: Cain Marko (Juggernaut)
options.description:
He's the juggernaut, bitch!

Cain Marko is possibly the most villainous of the villains listed here, being kind of an asshole in addition to being a bad guy. 

That being said, he's also incredibly powerful, so don't say that to his face. Brutally strong, nigh-indestructible, and resistant to almost everything. He lives up to his name

options.label: Quentin Beck
options.description:
Another Spidey villain, Beck is a but of a loopy one, without significant powers of his own, he uses old-school illusions and suggestions. Even hypnotism to make things seem like they're going his way.

I would not recommend taking Mysterio with anyone even tangentially related to Spider-Man. He might use you as a way to get to him.

options.label: Erik Lehnsherr (Magneto)
options.description:
Arguably the most powerful person in this entire CYOA, Magneto probably isn't going to be your friend. Building a relationship with him is more likely to just be joining the Brotherhood of Mutants or whatever he's running on any given day. Since the people he's actually close to can be counted on one hand. 

Doesn't stop him from being the favorite of a lot of people though.

options.label: Gwen Stacy
options.description:
One of Spidey's some-time girlfriends. This version of Gwen got bitten. (That's the multiverse at work), and became Spider-Gwen, sometimes known as Ghost-Spider, instead of Peter Parker. 

She's got all his powers, but I am not gonna lie, her quip game isn't quite as strong as his.

options.label: Neena Thurman (Domino)
options.description:
I almost put Domino in the reality warpers category. That's how lucky she is. It's a bona-fide super power. No matter how strange it seems. 

Beyond that, she's a heck of a merc. Shilled with all sorts of weaponry, and just full of quips, she's kind of like a female version of myself, without the healing factor.

options.label: Beta-Ray Bill
options.description:
If you ever need someone to beat down Thor, just call BRB here. He wiped the floor with Thor and stole Mjolnir. Such an impressive feat that Odin made him a new weapon of his own so he could give Thor's weapon back.

He's worthy of Asguardian semi-sentient weapons, so you can infer a fair bit about him based on that alone.

options.label: Henry "Hank" Pym
options.description:
Hank's been more heroes than anybody else I know. And that's saying something. More impressively, he doesn't even have any powers. He invented all of those heroes' tech himself. Like Iron Man, only more versatile. 

He's renounced most hero work these days though, so he's mostly just a researcher and scientist.

options.label: Greer Nelson (Tigra)
options.description:
Tigra's one of the more obscure members of the Avengers, but that doesn't make her any less popular, and can you blame people? Just look at her!

She's got feline reflexes, strength, claws, and tendencies, including bathing herself with her tongue and hating water.

options.label: T'Challa (Black Panther)
options.description:
King of Wakanda and serious badass, T'Challa is a wicked hand-to-hand marital artist, able to keep up with Captain America, even with his shield.

He's very serious and regal most of the time, but more than that, he's extremely dedicated to his people, which includes you if you're among those he calls friend.

options.label: Frank Castle (Punisher)
options.description:
Frank Castle's had a hard life, man. He's got PTSD on top of PTSD, but he soldiers on through it.

He also doesn't have any powers, but he has an absolutely absorb amount of weaponry, and a determination and a sense of justice that are strong enough to withstand ghost rider's penance stare.

options.label: Andrea Benton (Mania)
options.description:
A female non-murderous venom Symbiote. Andrea is a temperamental teen with a kickass set of abilities, making her a real problem for her enemies. 

She is/was a partner of Agent Venom over there, so she's no only reliant on the symbiote for combat, but has some second-hand military combat training

options.label: Marc Spector (Moon-Knight)
options.description:
Heavy-weight boxer turned US Marine turned avatar of an ancient Egyptian God, Marc (who rarely let's people know who he really is) is a wickedly talented fighter and crime-fighter. 

He's a tad melodramatic for my tastes, and takes himself rather seriously as the 'fist of konshu' but he's definitely one of the good guys.

options.label: Flash Thompson
options.description:
A Spec-Ops soldier who lost his legs while being a total badass in wartime, the military used the venom symbiote to not only restore his lost potential, but make him an even more lethal weapon than before. 

He's disciplined and good-natured, having been a gym-teacher for Andrea Benton's school in the past. though he now is focused entirely on being a hero.

options.label: Jennifer Waters
options.description:
Bruce Banner's cousin or some such, she's got a blood transfusion from him that got her permanently stuck in a hulk form. She's not usually quite as strong as him, but she does still get stronger when she gets angrier, so watch out. 

She's also a lawyer in her day job, and a damn good one too, so watch out. Doubly so.

options.label: Miles Morales
options.description:
Man, Spider-People are just popping up left and right. Miles is a replacement for Peter Parker. But, he's a good kid despite that, and thanks to time shenanigans, we can have him and Peter at the same time. 

Just like Gwen, he's just an alternate skin for the Spidey character, powers wise.

description:
So, you figured out your powers, who you wanted to train under (heh), and what sort of gear you had. Now that you are more of an established super... Wait, did you end up being a hero or a villain? You know what? It doesn't mater. I've got stuff for everyone here. 

I've got people here looking for all manner of partners. Crime-fighting partners, romantic partners, world domination partners, even just friendly partners. You do not have all the free time in the world, nor, let's face it, are you the most charismatic person the world has ever seen. So [color=red]you will only be able to manage up to four stable relationships[/color] of any kind, plus some bonus ones. I'll explain later on in this section. Anyways, here are the dossiers I've assembled!

options.label: Unmasked
options.description:
You do not get a secret identity. You never have. As soon as you got your powers, it seems like everyone and their dog found out about them, and you were the highlight of every paper. 

This extends to any aliases you try to establish as well. Disguises don't seem to work as well for you and false identities get exposed or fall apart almost immediately.

options.label: Orphaned
options.description:
'Orphan' isn't a strong enough word. Orphans can have aunts, uncles, cousins, extended family, etc. You can't. Not only are your parents and the rest of your family dead, but bad things tend to happen to new family members you try to gain.

You can have friends and partners (though 'loner' can take care of that). But if you start to consider someone like an adoptive parent to you? Keep a close eye on them.

options.label: Obligations
options.description:
You have a family, friend group, or some other obligation that depends on you for their continued survival. This cuts into your financial freedom, your time, and provides a weakness that villains can exploit. 

You cannot take this drawback then disregard your obligations. You must be committed to the well-being of the family that relies on you.

options.label: Loner
options.description:
You lose all your base companion points that you normally set, and you cannot use this drawback to gain two additional companions. You don't work well with others and can't join teams except in cases of dire emergencies, and I mean dire. Like, world-ending crises.

This only applies to other heroes. You can still take obligations and have civilian friends.

options.label: Vulnerability
options.description:
Much like the symbiotes' vulnerability to fire and sound, you have a weakness that bypasses any durability you have. Even durability granted by transformative items, and which weakens any other powers you have while you are exposed to it. 

You can choose the exact nature of your weakness, but it must be something that can reasonably be brought to bear against you by other supers.

options.label: Deep Trauma
options.description:
Something in your past, or many somethings have left you mentally (and possibly physically) scarred. This traums is not enough for a real drawback. However, this drawback includes unhealthy coping mechanisms such as alcoholism or addiction to hard drugs. 

You wake up in a cold sweat at least once a week and your public image will be significantly harder to maintain.

options.label: Persecution
options.description:
The public, or at least a large portion of it, hates you and your kind. It makes little to no distinction between different super origins. Hating Dr. Strange, Spider-Man, and Wolverine equally.

Being super-powered, you're unlikely to be attacked personally, but life will be harder. People will be ungrateful, even when you save them, and your civilian life and obligations will be harassed at all times.

options.label: Sacred Vow
options.description:
Like Samson (from the Bible), your powers are derived from a divine source, and your continued use of those powers is dependent on a vow or deal you have made with that divinity. 

You can choose the exact nature of this vow, but it must be restrictive, along the lines of a vow of chastity, vow of permanent poverty, or a vowa of abstinence from drugs or alcohol.

options.label: A Nation of One's Own
options.description:
So, you want the Doctor Doom School of Power. You are not happy with just technological, physical, or magical power. You need political power as well. This is a long, hard road process to go through, even for the most powerful and charismatic of supers. Most of the civilized world is more or less content with their leadership, and they won't take kindly to someone swooping in to take over. 

Expect to spend years in legal controversies, committee discussions, and long discussions with bureaucrats. But, at the end of that long, dark tunnel, you will be the king of your own little (or, heck not-so-little) kingdom.

options.label: To Court Death
options.description:
Aww, Man. Come on! After all I do for you, you're gonna try and woo my girl! 

I mean, I can't blame you. My bone-mama is the hottest there is! The Mad Titan Thanos though so too. Even though she kept dissing his purple ass when he kept killing people for her. 

Anyway, I do not really have any advice for you about this one. With her and me, it sort of just happened, and as Thanos proved, killing half of the universe (or half of several universes even). Isn't necessarily enough to win her favor. Good Luck. You're gonna need it.

options.label: An Army of One
options.description:
The multiverse is a big place kid, and you aren't the only you in it. Some of these versions of you are gonna be incompatible with your goals and ideals, but a lot of them are gonna be just "you" with a different paint job. If you want, you can collect more or less infinite numbers of yourself, forming, as the quest title indicates, an army of one. 

You will probably spend most of your time bouncing around the multiverse. Once your figure out how to bounce around the multiverse, and you will need to provide assistance to your other dimensional selves. just like they will be providing assistance to you.

options.label: They Came from Beyond!
options.description:
The world(s) seem to suffer invasions from aliens on a regular basis. If you dedicate yourself to fighting the Skrull, Kree, Celestials, and other alien races that seem hell-bent on messing with us guys who are just trying to live our lives, you can unlock the secrets of technology that normally only guys like Reed and Doom can understand. 

This quest actually provides a mechanical benefit other than "you get to be with death" or "You get a kingdom." With this quest, you can unlock the second tier of transformative items. The stuff that normally requires you to have alien tech as a power.

options.label: The Strongest There Is
options.description:
Some people like to focus on their powers to the exclusion of all else. You train and train and train. And you do almost nothing else but train and fight and train, and maybe study depending how you get your powers. 

This does not have any mechanical benefits, like more points or anything, but the powers you already have will get substantially more potent if you have the second tier purchased.

options.label: Peace on Earth
options.description:
"An on Earth, Peace, and goodwill towards men" 

Truly the most noble of quests, but probably also the hardest. Especially if you have also taken the persecution drawback. This quest represents an ongoing struggle to end oppression and war. To unify all peoples in harmony. 

Honestly, probably a futile objective for even the most dedicated super to accomplish on their own. I would advise you find as many like-minded heroes as possible to join forces with you. Many hands make light work, as my nana always said.
